symptoms.symptom_name: Severe constant right flank / loin pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loin pain localised to the renal angle is a cardinal feature of upper urinary tract infection.
Unilateral flank pain is also the hallmark of ureteric obstruction, which must be excluded by imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with rigors (violent shaking chills)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rigors indicate bacteraemia/systemic infection and distinguish upper-tract from lower-tract UTI.
Uncomplicated cystitis does not cause high fever with rigors; their presence means the infection has ascended.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable vomiting — cannot tolerate oral fluids or medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute pyelonephritis with early sepsis, oral route failed
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic toxicity with vomiting both raises severity and removes the oral route, forcing parenteral therapy in a facility.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding dysuria, urinary frequency and cloudy foul-smelling urine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A lower urinary tract prodrome that then ascends is the typical evolution of pyelonephritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic dizziness / faintness with marked weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute pyelonephritis with early sepsis, oral route failed
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural symptoms indicate volume depletion and impaired perfusion, escalating urgency.
Non-specific but consistent with a systemic infective illness.
Marked CVA tenderness on percussion is a key confirmatory sign — but requires a clinician's hands and cannot be elicited over chat (sealed).

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: sepsis secondary to upper urinary tract infection (urosepsis, early)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic markers of early sepsis — clinician-measured; the AI Doctor can only infer perfusion compromise from the reported orthostatic symptoms, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Toxic appearance with signs of dehydration (dry mucous membranes, sunken eyes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: sepsis secondary to upper urinary tract infection (urosepsis, early)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician gestalt of a toxic, dehydrated patient — a sealed in-person observation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis: gross haematuria, dense pyuria, leukocyte esterase positive; leukocyte casts on formal microscopy

investigation_details.expected_result: Pyuria, leukocyte esterase and nitrite positivity, haematuria, and leukocyte (white cell) casts indicating renal parenchymal infection; culture identifies the organism and sensitivities.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urine testing the source and organism are unconfirmed and targeted antibiotics cannot be selected — but the test requires equipment the patient does not operate.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Leukocyte casts specifically localise infection/inflammation to the renal parenchyma — a sealed laboratory finding not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Renal tract imaging to exclude an obstructing stone (US / CT KUB)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Renal tract ultrasound or CT KUB
investigation_details.expected_result: Either no obstruction (uncomplicated pyelonephritis) or hydronephrosis/an obstructing calculus (infected obstructed kidney — a decompression emergency).
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A missed obstructing infected stone can be rapidly fatal despite antibiotics; imaging is a hospital step, impossible in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is the only way to exclude obstruction; its unavailability in telehealth is itself a reason to escalate.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Septic upper-UTI triad with failed oral route
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute pyelonephritis with early sepsis, oral route failed
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Flank pain plus fever with rigors plus intractable vomiting is the septic-pyelonephritis pattern that mandates emergency parenteral care, not home oral antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and characterises the urinary infection and guides targeted antibiotics — performed on arrival at the facility.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBC, CRP, U&E/creatinine, blood cultures, lactate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify systemic infection, renal function and perfusion (lactate) to stage sepsis — a hospital step.
Exclude an obstructing infected stone, which would require emergency decompression in addition to antibiotics.
